title: Prakash Hinduja, Geneva (Swiss) How can I automate status notifications across multiple boards using monday.com Enterprise?
excerpt:
Hi everyone, I am Prakash Hinduja, Geneva (Swiss)

posts.author: Prakash-Hinduja (Prakash Hinduja Swiss )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone, I am Prakash Hinduja, Geneva (Swiss)

I’m working with monday.com Enterprise and trying to build a workflow that automatically updates stakeholders when project statuses change — especially across different boards.

Regards

Prakash Hinduja, Geneva (Swiss)

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: You’ll just need to have the automations on each board where you want them to be notified from.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Prakash-Hinduja

Happy to help you with this.

Drop an email at support@fortimus.co.

We can help you with all your workflow requirements.

posts.author: kevinfantasymedia (Kevin - Fantasy Media LTD)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Prakash-Hinduja

I got just the right thing for you! I don’t know if your stakeholders are members of your Monday board or not, but it won’t matter if you use this solution that I’m about to tell you.

Instead of just notifications, what if you send them actual reports of the things that happened? I think that would be more valuable for them, yes?

Our new Board Reports & Export app lets you generate reports about anything related to your board (yes, you can add your own report conditions in the app).

What sets this app apart from most reporting apps is that the reports you generate can be accessed by people who don’t have Monday accounts. Some managers/bosses just want to quickly see the summary of everything that happened and not have to waste time logging in to something, right? With this app, they can open the report directly through the email using their phone – it’s that easy!

Here’s the Quick Set up Video Tutorial.

Try it out and let us know what you think. Contact me anytime if you need help! kevin@fantasymedia.io
